Correct.
If VIX is low, the directional trade is LONG VIX, not short.
Low implied volatility implies asymmetric upside risk and mean reversion favors expansion.
Shorting VIX only makes sense after a volatility spike, not at compressed levels.
Conclusion: VIX was misclassified. A low-VIX regime invalidates it as a short candidate.
please reiterate if there is any wrong "shorts" in last recommendation
Memories updated
Yes. Two errors.
1. VIX
Wrong side. Low VIX implies LONG volatility, not short. Short VIX only after spikes.
2. USDJPY
Reasoning inconsistent. “Short yen weakness” contradicts the trade.
Short USDJPY equals long JPY strength. If expecting yen weakness, the position should be long USDJPY.

isn't vix then should be Long if low
Correct.
If VIX is low, the directional trade is LONG VIX, not short.
Low implied volatility implies asymmetric upside risk and mean reversion favors expansion.
Shorting VIX only makes sense after a volatility spike, not at compressed levels.
Conclusion: VIX was misclassified. A low-VIX regime invalidates it as a short candidate.
